channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Spurs survival celebrations can't disguise season of embarrassment
item.description: Tottenham Hotspur's celebrations in survival cannot cover up their embarrassment of a season that almost ended in the unthinkable, says chief football writer Phil McNulty.

item.title: The moment that broke Guardiola on day a decade of glory ended
item.description: Pep Guardiola was playing it cool in his final game in charge of Manchester City - until the magnitude of the day gripped him on the hour mark.

item.title: What happens now at relegated West Ham?
item.description: West Ham's 14-year stay in the Premier League is over - but what changes lie ahead as they prepare for life in the Championship?

item.title: Could title win start period of dominance for Arsenal?
item.description: It's been a long wait but after 22 years, Arsenal finally have their hands on the Premier League trophy once more.

item.title: League One to Europa League in four years - Sunderland defy odds
item.description: When Trai Hume signed for Sunderland for just £200,000 when they were in League One, he would not have in his wildest dreams thought he would be scoring to send the club into Europe.

item.title: Champions League secured - what's next for Slot and Liverpool?
item.description: Liverpool have crept over the line to secure Champions League qualification a year after winning the title, so what comes next for boss Arne Slot?

item.title: Iraola signs off after writing new Bournemouth chapter
item.description: Andoni Iraola leaves Bournemouth having taken the Cherries into Europe.

item.title: Spurs secure Premier League survival with win over Everton
item.description: Joao Palhinha's goal just before half time helps Tottenham overcome Everton to secure the result they needed to ensure Premier League survival on a day of nerve-shredding tension.

item.title: West Ham relegated despite win over Leeds
item.description: West Ham's 14-year stay in the Premier League comes to an end after they were relegated to the Championship on the final day of the season despite beating Leeds at London Stadium.

item.title: Arsenal beat Palace before lifting Premier League trophy
item.description: Arsenal start their reign as Premier League champions with a routine win over Crystal Palace in a party atmosphere at Selhurst Park.

item.title: Sunderland beat Chelsea to qualify for Europa League
item.description: Sunderland beat Chelsea to qualify for European competition for the first time in 53 years, securing a place in the Europa League at the visitors' expense.

item.title: Fernandes breaks Premier League record as Man Utd beat Brighton
item.description: Manchester United beat Brighton on the final day of the season, with captain Bruno Fernandes breaking the Premier League record for most assists in a single campaign.

item.title: Who has made Troy's Premier League team of the season?
item.description: After every round of Premier League matches this season, Troy Deeney gives us his team of the week. This time, it's team of the season. Do you agree with his choices?

item.title: Conte to leave Napoli a year after winning Serie A
item.description: Napoli manager Antonio Conte announces he will leave the Italian club this summer, 12 months after winning Serie A.

item.title: Torreense stun Sporting to win Portuguese Cup
item.description: Second division side Torreense stun holders Sporting to win the Portuguese Cup with a penalty in extra time sealing a 2-1 victory.

item.title: Which Premier League clubs have qualified for Europe?
item.description: Liverpool, Bournemouth, Sunderland and Brighton all secure European qualification on the Premier League's final day.

item.title: Watkins & Villa spoil Guardiola's final Man City match
item.description: Pep Guardiola's 593rd and final game in charge of Manchester City ended in an emotional Premier League defeat as Ollie Watkins scored a double for Aston Villa.

item.title: Liverpool confirm Champions League spot with draw against Brentford
item.description: Liverpool confirm Champions League football for next season despite being held by Brentford as Anfield waved farewell to club legends Mohamed Salah and Andy Robertson.

item.title: Gibbs-White sends England message as Forest draw with Bournemouth
item.description: Nottingham Forest's Morgan Gibbs-White sends a message to England boss Thomas Tuchel, as Bournemouth miss out on the Champions League.

item.title: Fulham finish season with comfortable win over Newcastle
item.description: Substitute Thomas Cairney scores a superb long-range strike as Fulham signed off with a comfortable win against Newcastle United on the final day of the Premier League season.

item.title: Burnley and Wolves end Premier League campaigns with draw
item.description: Burnley and Wolves play out a 1-1 draw at Turf Moor to close out a miserable Premier League campaign for the two sides, who were relegated well in advance of the final day.

item.title: Watch: Arsenal lift Premier League trophy
item.description: Watch Arsenal lift the Premier League trophy as champions of the 2025-26 season at Selhurst Park following their win over Crystal Palace.

item.title: How 'electric' Watkins forced his way back into Tuchel's England
item.description: Match of the Day pundits Alan Shearer and Wayne Rooney praise Aston Villa's Ollie Watkins on his "unbelievable" hot streak since he was left out of the England squad in March.

item.title: West Ham deserve to be in the Premier League - Nuno
item.description: West Ham's Nuno Espirito Santo expresses his disappointment at the club's relegation despite a valiant effort on the final day of the Premier League season.

item.title: I'm very happy, I'm very delighted - De Zerbi
item.description: Tottenham's Roberto De Zerbi says he's already working on the next season as they survive relegation to stay in the Premier League.

item.title: Man Utd target West Ham's Fernandes - Monday's gossip
item.description: Manchester United target West Ham's Mateus Fernandes and Atalanta's Ederson, Tottenham chase Paulo Dybala and Nottingham Forest seek Middlesbrough's Hayden Hackney, plus more.

item.title: From amateurs to SWPL champions - how Olid transformed Hearts
item.description: Eva Olid led Hearts to the Scottish Women's Premier League title, but she departs now after a five-year transformation of the team.

item.title: Como qualify for Champions League for first time while AC Milan & Juventus miss out
item.description: Cesc Fabregas's Como qualify for the Champions League for the first time in their history with a 4-1 victory over 10-man Cremonese on the final day of the Serie A season.

item.title: Rangers agree deal for Hearts captain Shankland
item.description: Rangers agree a deal for Hearts captain Lawrence Shankland, subject to a medical, which will take place in the coming days.

item.title: How well do you remember this season's Premier League?
item.description: Test your knowledge of the 2025-26 Premier League season before the final round of fixtures.

item.title: Mamelodi Sundowns win African Champions League title
item.description: South Africa's Mamelodi Sundowns clinch their second African Champions League title with a 2-1 aggregate win over Moroccan club AS FAR in the final.

item.title: Could history be rewritten? Guardiola, Man City and the 115 charges
item.description: With Pep Guardiola leaving Manchester City at the end of this season, how do the club's 115 Premier League charges for alleged financial rule breaches potentially affect his legacy at Etihad Stadium?

item.title: Saying goodbye to Football Focus after 52 years
item.description: After Football Focus broadcasts its final episode after 52 years on the air, former presenters and pundits reminisce about the good times.

item.title: Wembley, Vegas, Premier League - jubilant Hull back in the big time
item.description: Even the most dedicated Hull City fan could not have envisaged their season ending like this, with Wembley glory and a return to the top flight.

item.title: From Chelsea relegation to £200m game - how play-offs have evolved
item.description: From crowds of 15,000 watching on 40 years ago to the richest game in world football today, the play-offs have changed significantly.

item.title: Guardiola's legacy will echo through all ages for years to come
item.description: As Manchester City prepare for Pep Guardiola's exit, Phil McNulty reflects on how he has changed football in England.

item.title: Ultimate image for Saudi Arabia's football poster boy: Ronaldo wins title
item.description: Cristiano Ronaldo wins his first league title with Al-Nassr after three years in Saudi Arabia.

item.title: All done deals since February, including deadline day
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: Who are the oldest players to feature at a World Cup?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at who the oldest players are in the history of the World Cup

item.title: Who has qualified for next season's Champions League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains which teams have already qualified for the 2026-27 edition of the Champions League.

item.title: Who has the most Premier League assists in a season?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team look at who has provided the most assists in the history of the Premier League.

item.title: What does Premiership climax mean for European places?
item.description: What does Saturday's dramatic and chaotic climax to the Scottish Premiership top-six mean for European football next season?

item.title: All you need to know about the Europa League final
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at all you need to know before this year's Europa League final.

item.title: What is financial gap between Old Firm and rest of Scottish Premiership?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team look at the financial gap between the Old Firm and the rest of Scottish top-flight football.

item.title: What are Fifa's rules on switching nationalities?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains the rules on players switching nations in international football.

item.title: Hull's Wembley hero McBurnie - a striker Scotland can do without?
item.description: Hull City striker Oli McBurnie sends a message to Scotland boss Steve Clarke with his Championship play-off final winner after being omitted from the World Cup.

item.title: What does WSL relegation mean for Leicester City?
item.description: After a five-year stay in the WSL, Leicester City were relegated on Saturday - so what does the future of the club look like?

item.title: Whitehouse's penalty masterclass sends Charlton into WSL
item.description: Sophie Whitehouse is the ‘star of the show’ as Charlton Athletic reach the Women’s Super League for the first time.

item.title: Germany forward Martinez to join Spurs from West Ham
item.description: Tottenham Hotspur agree to sign Germany forward Shekiera Martinez from West Ham.

item.title: The summer a golden generation lost its shine
item.description: The summer of 2006 was meant to be England's moment. A world-class squad, celebrity partners and confident fans - the hype was huge and the fall was hard.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.

item.title: Captain, player, manager, Mister, number one - Davids at Barnet
item.description: BBC Sport remembers Edgar Davids' 15 months as Barnet player-manager in League Two and the Conference.

item.title: From farming village to Liverpool icon - the Salah story
item.description: BBC Sport visits Egypt to discover what Mohamed Salah means to the people of the football-mad country, and how a small boy from humble beginnings became a national icon.
